rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH M BREALL VS. TAXI COMMISSION, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Admit Supplemental Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 25, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH BREALL'S Motion To Admit Supplemental Evidence is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH M BREALL VS. TAXI COMMISSION, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Grant Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 25, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH BREALL'S Motion To Grant Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is denied. The court, applying the substantial evidence standard, finds that the Board did not proceed in excess of its jurisdiction, provided a fair trial on the issues, and did not abuse its discretion in its findings and judgment. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES THOMPSON et al VS. MARY BEECROFT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Permitting Amendment Of Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 25, 2011, line 14. PLAINTIFFS JAMES THOMPSON, ANTHONY PINO'S Motion For Order Permitting Amendment Of Complaint is denied. First, plaintiffs "new evidence" was known to plaintiff at all times. Second, the changes are quite broad in their scope by seeking damages over a 5 year period of time (outside the applicable statute of limitations). Further, this matter is set for trial in two weeks and defendant would be prejudiced by all of these factors. =(302/LMG)
